title: galatella sedifolia subsp. biflora
intro: Galatella sedifolia subsp. biflora, also known as the bifid Galatella, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family. It is used in herbal medicine, particularly in traditional European folk remedies, for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including the ability to soothe digestive issues and relieve respiratory problems, and it has been traditionally used as a treatment for colds, fever, and skin irritations. Galatella sedifolia subsp. biflora can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, allowing for the extraction of its active compounds and utilization in various herbal remedies.
